questions.questionText: Which hook is used to manage local state in a React functional component?
What does the useEffect hook with an empty dependency array do?
options: Runs only when specific state changes

questions.questionText: Which method is called when a class component is removed from the DOM?
What is the purpose of React.memo?
options: To prevent re-rendering of a component when props haven't changed
questions.correctAnswer: To prevent re-rendering of a component when props haven't changed

questions.questionText: What is the difference between useCallback and useMemo?
options: useCallback memoizes a function; useMemo memoizes a computed value
useCallback memoizes values; useMemo memoizes functions
useMemo is only for class components
questions.correctAnswer: useCallback memoizes a function; useMemo memoizes a computed value

questions.questionText: In the React reconciliation process, what key prop is used to help React identify list items?
Which pattern allows components to share stateful logic without changing the component hierarchy?
Which decorator is used to define an Angular component?
What is the Angular CLI command to generate a new component?
What type of binding is used with [(ngModel)]?
Which Angular feature is responsible for intercepting HTTP requests and responses?
What is the purpose of the ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush in Angular?
options: To trigger change detection on every cycle
To only run change detection when inputs change or events are fired
To schedule change detection for the next microtask
questions.correctAnswer: To only run change detection when inputs change or events are fired

questions.questionText: In Angular 17 standalone components, which function is used to lazy-load a component in routing?
What is the role of NgRx Effects in an Angular application?
options: To handle side effects like HTTP calls triggered by actions
To register reducers in the store
questions.correctAnswer: To handle side effects like HTTP calls triggered by actions

questions.questionText: Which keyword declares a variable that cannot be reassigned?
What does the spread operator (...) do when used with arrays?
options: Creates a shallow copy or expands elements
Converts an array to a string
questions.correctAnswer: Creates a shallow copy or expands elements

questions.questionText: What is the output of: typeof null?
What does Promise.all() do when one of the promises rejects?
options: Returns undefined for the rejected promise
Waits for all promises to settle

questions.questionText: What is the event loop's microtask queue used for?
What does the logical nullish assignment operator (??=) do?
options: Assigns only if the left operand is falsy
Assigns only if the left operand is null or undefined
questions.correctAnswer: Assigns only if the left operand is null or undefined

questions.questionText: Which access modifier in Java makes a member accessible only within the same class?
What is the difference between an interface and an abstract class in Java?
options: An interface can have concrete methods; an abstract class cannot
An abstract class can have state and concrete methods; an interface only has abstract methods (prior to Java 8)
questions.correctAnswer: An abstract class can have state and concrete methods; an interface only has abstract methods (prior to Java 8)

questions.questionText: Which Java collection maintains insertion order and allows duplicates?
What is the result of Integer.parseInt("abc") in Java?
Which garbage collection algorithm divides heap into Young and Old generations?
What is the difference between == and .equals() for String comparison in Java?
options: == checks reference equality; .equals() checks value equality
== checks value equality; .equals() checks reference equality
questions.correctAnswer: == checks reference equality; .equals() checks value equality

questions.questionText: Which SQL clause filters rows after aggregation?
What type of JOIN returns all rows from the left table and matching rows from the right?
Which SQL statement is used to prevent reading uncommitted data in a transaction?
What is a covering index?
options: An index on a foreign key
An index that includes all columns needed by a query, avoiding a table lookup
A composite index on all table columns
An index that covers NULL values
questions.correctAnswer: An index that includes all columns needed by a query, avoiding a table lookup

questions.questionText: What does the COALESCE function do in SQL?
options: Returns the maximum value in a column
Returns the first non-NULL value from a list of arguments
questions.correctAnswer: Returns the first non-NULL value from a list of arguments

questions.questionText: Which SQL window function assigns a unique sequential integer to each row within a partition?
Which keyword enables strict type checking in TypeScript?
What is the TypeScript utility type that makes all properties optional?
What does the 'keyof' operator do in TypeScript?
options: Returns all values of an object
Creates a union type of all property keys of a type
questions.correctAnswer: Creates a union type of all property keys of a type

questions.questionText: What is a discriminated union in TypeScript?
options: A union type with no common properties
A union type where each member has a common literal property used to narrow the type
A type that combines multiple interfaces
questions.correctAnswer: A union type where each member has a common literal property used to narrow the type

questions.questionText: What is the difference between 'interface' and 'type' in TypeScript?
options: Interfaces can be extended; types cannot
Interfaces support declaration merging; type aliases do not
Types are preferred for object shapes; interfaces for primitives
questions.correctAnswer: Interfaces support declaration merging; type aliases do not
